tier: H7 / H-g + H-h: big-endian HFS+ journal replay + HFS wrapper write
host: Big-endian journal replay is unit byte-equality certified (no Apple PowerPC big-endian rig exists, so byte-equality vs the certified little-endian direct-write path is the H-g exit gate). The HFS wrapper write is certified with Apple's own diskdev_cmds 540.1 fsck_hfs (built in WSL archlinux at /root/ddcbuild) on a real `newfs_hfs -w` wrapped volume that S.A.K. mutated.
change: H-g (big-endian journal replay): the journal replay engine (partition_hfs_internal.h) now decodes a journal whose endian marker (offset 4) selects big-endian, reading every journal-header / block-list-header / block-info field in the journal's own byte order (new ju16/ju32/ju64 + writeJu32/writeJu64 dispatch on a per-state big_endian flag set from the endian magic; new writeBe64 in partition_hfs_core.h). The journaled block PAYLOADS are copied verbatim either way, and the clean-journal write-back stamps the start pointer + checksum in the journal's native endian. Previously big-endian journals were fail-closed. H-h (HFS wrapper write): confirmed the write path under the embedded-volume offset -- detection (loadWrappedVolumeHeader) already sets m_volume.volume_offset to the embedded HFS+ start and every read/write/alternate-header-sync adds it, gated fail-closed behind allow_wrapped_volume; this gate exercises + certifies it end to end.
evidence_unit: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp: hfsFileSystemWriter_replaysBigEndianJournal builds the SAME single-block transaction as the certified little-endian replay test but with every journal header / block-list / block-info field big-endian, replays it, and asserts the replayed data region is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the certified direct-write path (the H-g exit gate), plus read-back == new content, a clean big-endian header (start == end), a no-op re-replay, and a tampered big-endian transaction checksum that fails closed without writing. hfsFileSystemWriter_writesIntoWrappedVolume embeds a complete writable HFS+ volume at the wrapper offset behind a 'BD' master directory block, writes a file via overwriteFileSameSizeFromImage, and asserts: fail-closed without allow_wrapped_volume (image untouched), then with the override the data lands INSIDE the embedded volume (offset by the wrapper start) and round-trips, the wrapper MDB is untouched, and nothing is written at the non-wrapped offset-0 position. Core suite 150/150; clang-format / lizard / cppcheck clean.

evidence_wrapper_fsck.create: Apple diskdev_cmds 540.1 `newfs_hfs -w -v SAKH7W wrapped.img` (32 MiB) created a real HFS wrapper (MDB 'BD' @ byte 1024) around an embedded HFS+ volume (H+ header @ byte 0x7000).
evidence_wrapper_fsck.baseline: `fsck_hfs -d -n wrapped.img` on the freshly wrapped volume: '** Checking non-journaled HFS Plus Volume', catalog / catalog-hierarchy / multi-linked / extents / attributes / bitmap all checked, '** The volume SAKH7W appears to be OK.'
evidence_wrapper_fsck.sak_mutation: `sak_hfs_writer_cli create-empty-file-image --target wrapped.img --hfs-path /sak-wrapped.txt --allow-wrapped-volume` -> ok=true, warnings: 'HFS wrapper volume was detected', 'HFS+ empty file created...', 'HFS+ alternate volume header synchronized'. The new catalog name (UTF-16BE 'sak-wrapped.txt') landed at byte offset 3092640, inside the embedded volume (>0x7000).
evidence_wrapper_fsck.post_mutation: `fsck_hfs -d -n wrapped.img` AFTER the S.A.K. write: catalog / catalog-hierarchy / multi-linked / extents / attributes / bitmap all checked, '** The volume SAKH7W appears to be OK.' -- the H-h exit gate.
